title: Explain LLM
short_answer: LLM is the language model block that generates text answers.
text: In this app, the LLM block is the central integration layer for text generation, prompts, model settings, and provider calls. Runners should use this layer instead of calling Ollama directly.

title: Explain Model
short_answer: A model is the AI engine that receives input and generates output.
text: The model is the part that actually produces text or vision answers. In this project, the text model can be configured separately from the vision model.

title: Explain LLM Settings
short_answer: llm_settings.py controls provider, models, timeouts, tokens, and temperature.
text: The llm_settings file defines how the app connects to the language model system. It should own model names, provider choice, timeout behavior, max tokens, and temperature.

title: Explain LLM Types
short_answer: llm_types.py defines request and result shapes for LLM calls.
text: The llm_types file keeps the input and output structures predictable. It helps services, clients, and runners agree on what a model request or response looks like.

title: Explain LLM Client
text: The LLM client is the low-level connector to the model provider, such as Ollama. Higher layers should not duplicate raw provider-call logic.

title: Explain LLM Service
short_answer: llm_service.py is the main entrypoint for text responses.
text: The LLM service should be what other parts of the app call when they need a model-generated text answer. It hides provider details behind a cleaner app-level function.

title: Explain Vision Service
short_answer: vision_service.py is the entrypoint for image or screenshot analysis.
text: The vision service should handle model calls that involve images. It keeps screenshot or vision analysis separate from normal text generation.

title: Explain LLM Prompts
short_answer: llm_prompts.py stores base prompts used by the LLM layer.
text: Prompts are the instructions given to the model. This file should hold reusable prompt text, not random runner logic.

title: Explain LLM Formatters
short_answer: llm_formatters.py shapes model output for UI and speech.
text: Formatters clean or shorten model output so it fits the destination. UI can accept more text, but speech often needs a shorter and clearer version.

title: Explain Ollama
short_answer: Ollama is the local provider used to run LLM models.
text: Ollama is one provider option for local model calls. The app should talk to it through app/llm instead of spreading Ollama-specific code across runners.
